author: sproul
content: Does anyone run the full-transition epoch processing tests? They seem a bit broken <https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/issues/5213>

author: nc1234
content: I’m OOO let me give a brief explanation shortly

author: nc1234
content: So take electra's `gossip_bls_to_execution_change__ignore_pre_capella` test for example, currently Lodestar initailizes the config for testing to be *_FORK_EPOCH to 0 for every fork before and including Electra. The message arrives at 0th (see `offset_ms`) second, so we interprett the message as arriving at 0th second of Electra fork so it is valid. In order to properly reject this pre-capella message, I would need to define BELLATRIX_FORK_EPOCH to 0, CAPELLA_FORK_EPOCH to 1, DENEB_FORK_EPOCH to 2, ELECTRA_FORK_EPOCH to 3, which the current gossip test case doesn't provide enough information to instruct us on how to initialize the chain config, and we just write some code just to deal with this one odd test case. And I don't foresee any upcoming gossip changes in gloas or heze to need this mechanism

author: jih2nn
content:
> currently Lodestar initailizes the config for testing to be *_FORK_EPOCH to 0 for every fork before and including Electra

Do you know if this is Lodestar-specific behavior? I wonder whether other clients also suffer from this issue, and if not, what makes it different.

author: potuz
content:
<@486501030046203915> <@968526635735326741> you guys mentioned a paper by  Malesh Pai on centralizing forces, are these the right references?
https://arxiv.org/abs/2408.03116  and the earlier https://arxiv.org/abs/2311.09083.

I'm starting to search the formal finance literature on the topic of repeated auctions to dispute the claim about the collapse of builders simply based on the assumption that not taking 100% of the builder stake is a loosing strategy vs trying to bid every slot.

author: jtraglia
content: Ended up dropping the `pre_epoch` and `post_epoch` outputs there. We could move the balance updates to the beginning of the epoch, but it would probably break the edge cases. See https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/5217

author: nero_eth
content:
As discussed on ACD, here's the informational EIP with the EL reorg depth. Posting it here for CL devs to verify the number (as it is based on non-finalization + inactivity leak with ~75% offline validators):
https://github.com/ethereum/EIPs/pull/11601

author: sproul
content: are there any ELs that are using the finalized block hash from forkchoiceUpdated to prune? I imagine more of them might want to rather than moving from a small constant like 128 blocks to 200k
